(Nasdaq: GP) (TSXV: GPV), a prominent manufacturer and supplier of all-electric, zero-emission medium and heavy-duty vehicles for the cargo and delivery sector, announced key developments. The company is known for its dedication to environmentally-friendly transportation solutions.

news.summary: On May 16, Executive Vice Chairman Chung Kisun engaged in official discussions with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer. The meeting focused on enhancing cooperation between Korea and the U.S. in the shipbuilding sector. HD Hyundai put forward a proposal for collaboration in port crane manufacturing. Chung emphasized the significance of U.S. shipbuilding, expressing a positive outlook on reinforcing ties in this realm.
news.headline: Hyundai's Vice Chairman met US Trade Rep. to discuss bilateral cooperation in shipbuilding, proposing collaboration on port crane manufacturing.

news.summary: Utah-based TruGolf Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: TRUG), a prominent provider of golf simulator technology, released its first quarter 2025 financial results. The company disclosed a revenue increase to $5.4 million, marking a 7.5% rise from the $5.0 million reported in the same period of 2024. However, net losses for the first quarter of 2025 doubled to ($2.6) million, compared to ($1.3) million in 2024, primarily due to interest expenses related to the conversion of convertible notes. In terms of earnings per share (EPS), the company saw an improvement to ($0.09) in the first quarter of 2025, up from ($0.22) in the corresponding period of 2024.
news.headline: TruGolf Q1 2025 sales increase by 7.5% to $5.4 million, but net losses double to ($2.6) million compared to ($1.3) million in Q1 2024. EPS improves to ($0.09) from ($0.22) in 2024.

news.summary: Federal prosecutors are pursuing a case against Tornado Cash founder Roman Storm but will drop a charge related to operating an unlicensed money transmitting business, aligning with the Department of Justice's recent rollback of crypto enforcement. The indictment will focus on Storm allegedly transmitting funds from criminal activities or to support illegal actions. Prosecutors claim Tornado Cash helped launder over $1 billion in crypto, including for the North Korean group Lazarus. Storm faces charges of money laundering conspiracy and violating US sanctions, each carrying up to 20 years in prison, and the unlicensed money transmitter conspiracy charge carries up to five years. Storm has pleaded not guilty, and his trial is set for July 14. Storm's associate Roman Semenov, believed to be in Russia, is also charged. Other crypto executives have cited the DOJ's shift in enforcement to seek dismissal of their charges, including Samourai Wallet's co-founders and SafeMoon's CEO.
news.headline: Tornado Cash founder's trial continues with minor adjustments due to DOJ policy change. $1 billion crypto laundered. Trial set for July 14.

news.summary: In Q1 2025, credit card lending in Hong Kong decreased by 5.8%, as reported by the Hong Kong Monetary Authority. This decline signifies a notable departure from the growth observed in the previous quarter.
news.headline: HKMA: Hong Kong credit card lending drops 5.8% in Q1 2025, reversing previous growth trend.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Tenaya Therapeutics Inc. (NASDAQ: TNYA) in South San Francisco granted stock options for 461,000 shares of common stock to three new non-executive employees as part of their employment commencement. The exercise price is set at $0.4373 per share, matching the closing price of Tenaya’s common stock on the same day. These stock options have a ten-year term and vest over four years: 1/4 will vest on the one-year anniversary of hire, and 1/48 will vest monthly afterward, contingent upon continued service with Tenaya.
news.headline: Tenaya Therapeutics granted 461,000 stock options worth $0.4373/share to 3 new employees; options vest over 4 years.

news.summary: In a recent event held in Seoul, South Korea on May 16, 2025, LG Electronics (LG) displayed a groundbreaking technology demonstrating satellite-based voice communication from a moving vehicle. This innovation highlights LG's position as a leading technology provider in the mobility sector. The showcase featured the utilization of Internet of Things - IoT solutions in enabling this seamless voice communication. This achievement marks a significant step towards enhancing safe and connected mobility experiences.
news.headline: LG showcases satellite-based connected car solution at 2025 5GAA conference in Seoul. Demonstrates voice communication from a moving vehicle. LG pioneers safe and connected mobility.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Shanghai-based company ArkBiopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. unveiled encouraging findings from the Phase II trial of their new anti-fibrotic medication AK3280 to tackle idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF). The study, overseen by Professor Huaping Dai, reported positive top-line results, indicating a significant step forward in the treatment of IPF. This development marks a crucial milestone for ArkBio as they strive to address the needs of patients with this debilitating condition.
news.headline: ArkBio reports positive Phase II results of AK3280 for IPF treatment led by Prof. Huaping Dai, Shanghai.

news.summary: In Saskatoon, Saskatchewan, ZYUS Life Sciences Corporation (TSX-V: ZYUS) has completed the second and final part of its non-brokered private placement. The Canadian life sciences company concentrates on creating and marketing innovative cannabinoid-based pharmaceutical drugs for pain relief. This announcement follows earlier news from May 1 and May 6, 2025. The completion of the Second Tranche marks a significant step in the Offering of units from the Company.
news.headline: ZYUS Life Sciences closes final tranche of unit offering, focused on cannabinoid-based drugs for pain management. TSX-V: ZYUS.

news.summary: Thousands of cyclists flooded the streets of San Francisco on May 15, 2025, to celebrate Bike to Wherever Day. The event saw tens of thousands of Bay Area residents participating in the regionwide celebration. This annual event is part of the Bay Area Bike to Wherever Days initiative, promoting cycling and sustainable transportation options. The exact number of participants was not disclosed, but the turnout was significant, highlighting the community's enthusiasm for biking. The event aimed to encourage more people to bike for commuting and recreation, promoting a healthier and more eco-friendly lifestyle.
news.headline: Bay Area sets record with tens of thousands of cyclists on May 15 Bike to Wherever Day celebration.

news.summary: In a press release dated May 15, 2025, Milwaukee-based Brady Corporation (NYSE: BRC) announced that its Board of Directors approved a dividend for holders of the company's Class A Common Stock. The declared dividend amounts to $0.24 per share and is scheduled to be paid on July 31, 2025. Shareholders recorded as owning the stock by the end of business on July 10, 2025, will be eligible to receive the dividend.
news.headline: Brady Corporation declares $0.24 dividend per share to shareholders of Class A Common Stock, payable on July 31, 2025.
